candidates.row_label: Net Foreign Exchange Income - Business Revenue
candidates.why: Candidate mentions revenue, but it is only one component of non-interest income and far too small to represent top-line revenue/operating income.
Clearly a balancing row, not a true revenue line.

variables.notes: Candidate list misses the apparent bank top-line. For a bank, the best available revenue-like line is 'Total operating income Operating expenses', which reconciles with operating profit when subtracting operating expenses and loan losses. Label is unusual/concatenated, so manual review is recommended. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Total operating income Operating expenses
No clear COGS/cost of sales line is present. This appears to be a bank income statement, where COGS is generally not applicable.

candidates.why: Not SG&A; this is a financial transactions subline.

Valid SG&A component, but appears to be a subcomponent of general administrative expenses rather than the primary reported SG&A line.

Headcount metric, not an expense row.

final_choice.row_label: Interest expenses to the general public

variables.notes: The statement provides multiple simultaneous interest expense components but no clear total interest expense line. Because XINT should be a single reported line and later code will not sum components, the best available row is used as a placeholder, but this requires manual review.

Not equity alone; includes liabilities.

variables.notes: No separate parent-equity or non-controlling-interest balance line is shown, so use total equity for BE and set MIB to empty to avoid double counting.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income - Attributable to Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Income statement attribution flow, not a balance sheet minority interest balance.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income before Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Not minority interest balance.

variables.notes: No balance sheet line for minority interest/non-controlling interests is present. Since BE uses total equity, MIB is left empty to avoid double counting.
